title: 76 School St Parking Lot Party Action Plan

location.building_note: Historic 1880 MBTA transfer station converted to residential/office lofts. Spring Hill neighborhood — Walk Score 92, highly walkable.
nearby_landmarks: Central Hill Memorial Park (~6 min walk)
Gilman Square MBTA Green Line (6 min walk)

pavement_condition.summary: Mixed conditions across the 300m radius area. Nearest scored segments (Putnam St and Prescott St) range from Very Poor (40) to Good (97+). Prescott Street, the closer artery, shows predominantly Good/Satisfactory PCI scores (78-97). Putnam Street shows more variability including Poor and Very Poor patches. Road surface adjacent to 76 School St is rated Satisfactory to Good (PCI 88-89 based on nearby imagery at 42.3850).
pavement_condition.access_roads_assessment: Satisfactory for vehicle access. Any parking lot surface itself would need independent inspection for foot traffic safety — look for settled asphalt, drainage, and uneven patches.
pavement_condition.foot_traffic_verdict: Adjacent public roads are suitable for pedestrian approach. A private lot would benefit from a walk-through inspection to identify trip hazards before event.

nearby_assets.note: Dense utility infrastructure. Multiple hydrants within 200m confirms good fire suppression access for event safety. Overhead utility poles may affect tent height and placement — survey required.

regulatory_categories.R7-2: No Parking, Any Time / Tow Away (10+ detected)

parking_signs_200m.interpretation: Heavy R7-x no-parking sign density confirms active parking enforcement zone. Event guests cannot use street parking immediately adjacent. Private lot must be secured as venue AND alternative guest parking arranged.
parking_signs_200m.sign_condition: All detected signs rated Good condition

closest_confirmed_imagery.note: Imagery at ~15m from 76 School St shows good pavement but some distress. Confirms active street use.

regulatory_requirements.agency: City of Somerville / Private Lot Owner
regulatory_requirements.timeline: Before any permit applications — secure first
regulatory_requirements.cost: $0 (or negotiated lot rental fee, est. $500–$2,000/day for private lot)
regulatory_requirements.details: Somerville requires evidence of property owner approval for any private property event. This is the foundational document everything else depends on.
regulatory_requirements.action: Contact lot owner; get signed letter on file

regulatory_requirements.timeline: Submit at least 13 days before next City Council meeting; plan 4-6 weeks total
regulatory_requirements.details: Required for events on private property that are open to the public or above certain size thresholds. Apply via Somerville CitizenServe portal under 'Public Event/Special Alcohol License' even if no alcohol. Email: cityclerk@somervillema.gov, Phone: 311.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: One-Day Special Alcohol License (if serving alcohol)
regulatory_requirements.cost: $100–$200 (MA ABCC comparable to Boston rate of $150/day all-alcohol)
regulatory_requirements.details: Required for any alcohol service at private events. Apply via CitizenServe under 'Public Event/Special Alcohol License'. Somerville Licensing Commission: 93 Highland Ave, (617) 625-6600 x4100. Hours: M-W 8:30-4:30, Thu 8:30-7:30, Fri 8:30-12:30.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Fire Prevention Bureau Permit (tents, crowds, open flame)
regulatory_requirements.details: Required if event includes tents over 200 sq ft, open flame/propane, candles, or anticipated crowd over 49 persons. Tent must be flame-retardant certified. No propane inside tents; propane must be 10ft+ from tent exits. Electric heat/lighting only inside structure. Apply online via CitizenServe.
regulatory_requirements.action: Call to confirm exact thresholds for your event size

regulatory_requirements.details: Required if food is prepared or served at the event (even by a caterer). Food trucks need their own permits. Handwashing station required if no running water on site.

Required if using a generator over 15kW or running temporary electrical service. 200m of the venue has 18+ utility poles and 16 street luminaries, suggesting potential for electrical utility hookup as alternative to generator — contact National Grid.
regulatory_requirements.mandatory: If using generator or temporary power
regulatory_requirements.action: Contact Inspectional Services; alternatively explore National Grid temporary hookup

regulatory_requirements.timeline: Plan during event; no permit needed but restrictions apply
regulatory_requirements.details: Massachusetts and Somerville follow MassDEP noise policy: ambient +10 dB at property line is a violation. General pattern: 70 dB max daytime, 50 dB max after 11pm. Outdoor music events should end amplified sound by 10pm in a residential neighborhood context. Spring Hill is mixed residential — neighbors are close.
regulatory_requirements.action: End amplified music by 10pm; hire sound engineer with dB meter

regulatory_requirements.timeline: Before all other permits — verify at planning stage
regulatory_requirements.details: Spring Hill is a mixed-use neighborhood. Somerville adopted a new form-based zoning code in 2019. Temporary outdoor events on private lots may need a zoning determination. Contact OSPCD to confirm the specific lot is not in a restricted residential zone.
regulatory_requirements.action: Email OSPCD with lot address and event description

regulatory_requirements.agency: City of Somerville (required for permit approval)
regulatory_requirements.details: Somerville typically requires general liability insurance of $1M per occurrence / $2M aggregate for public events. Property owner may also require additional insured status. Event insurers include NEXT Insurance, Markel, and similar specialty providers.
regulatory_requirements.action: Get event insurance quote online; add City of Somerville as additional insured

regulatory_requirements.details: If no indoor facilities accessible, portable toilets are required. Standard ratio: 1 unit per 50 guests for events under 4 hours; 1 per 75 for longer events. ADA-accessible unit required if event is open to public. Ensure unit placement does not block fire access or hydrants.
regulatory_requirements.action: Book rental 2+ weeks in advance; verify placement with lot owner

capacity_estimate.small_event_100_guests: Requires ~2,000 sq ft usable space minimum (20x100 ft lot); 100 guests standing with tables/DJ setup
capacity_estimate.medium_event_200_guests: Requires ~4,000 sq ft; 20x200 ft or 40x100 ft lot
capacity_estimate.large_event_300_guests: Requires ~6,000 sq ft; typical max for a private parking lot of 20-30 spaces
capacity_estimate.note: A standard 20-space parking lot (~8,000 sq ft) comfortably holds 150-250 guests with room for tent, DJ, bar, and dance floor

power_options.description: 20-50kW diesel generator for DJ, lighting, and catering equipment
power_options.cons: Noise, exhaust, requires permit if >15kW, fire separation distance required

power_options.description: Utility company provides temporary metered service from nearby pole
power_options.cons: Requires lead time (2-4 weeks), coordination with utility, utility poles confirmed within 200m of venue

power_options.description: Negotiate with building to tap existing electrical panels
power_options.cons: Depends on building owner cooperation and panel capacity

restroom_plan.cost_per_unit: $170–$300 per unit per event day (Boston area pricing)

waste_management.trash_removal: Include in vendor contract or self-haul
waste_management.note: 5 catch basins within 200m — protect storm drains from food waste per EPA regs

noise_constraints.dj_sound_limit: Keep at property line under 70 dB daytime, 50 dB post-11pm
noise_constraints.mitigation: Sound barriers (hay bales, acoustic drapes), aim speakers inward, hire sound engineer

guest_parking_plan.problem: The lot IS the venue; R7-x signs confirm heavy no-parking enforcement on surrounding streets. On-street parking unavailable for guests.

solutions.details: 6-minute walk from venue. Encourage transit. Post directions on invite.
Contact adjacent commercial properties (laundromats, churches, retail) for evening parking rights
Designate a Uber/Lyft staging area on the nearest legal curb
Valet service ferries cars to a remote lot 0.5–1 mile away

guest_parking_plan.recommendation: Lead with MBTA promotion. Supplement with 1 nearby overflow lot agreement.

cost_breakdown.scenario: 200-guest evening party, 5 hours, tent + DJ + bar + catering
cost_breakdown.note: Range $18,000–$30,000 depending on catering quality, alcohol choices, and lot rental negotiation

tasks: Confirm lot availability and sign rental agreement with property owner
Get written property owner approval letter (required for all permits)
Verify zoning compatibility with Somerville OSPCD (email planning@somervillema.gov)
Purchase event liability insurance — list City of Somerville as additional insured
Begin Public Event License application on CitizenServe
Submit Public Event License + Special Alcohol License applications
Book tent rental company (inventory fills fast in summer)
Book DJ and caterer
Identify and book portable toilet vendor
Negotiate overflow parking lot agreement
Submit Fire Prevention Bureau permit application
Submit Temporary Food Service permit (if applicable)
Submit electrical/generator permit
Confirm City Council meeting date for event license approval
Book security staff
Receive all permit approvals (follow up with each agency)
Confirm all vendor delivery times and load-in routes
Walk the lot with fire marshal for exit/egress layout
Print and post event signage and parking instruction cards
Final headcount to caterer
Confirm generator/power hookup
Walk the lot surface — mark any trip hazards with cones
Distribute MBTA directions and ride-share instructions to guests
Tent setup 8-10 hours before event
Generator/power test
Porta-potty delivery and positioning (not blocking hydrants)
Sound check (aim speakers inward, test at property line with dB meter)
Security briefing: crowd capacity, noise cutoff at 10pm, fire exits

emergency_access.hydrants_nearby: 8 fire hydrants confirmed within 200m radius via Cyvl data. Nearest hydrant IDs: 330, 989, 298, 299. Keep 15-foot clearance around all hydrants — no tent stakes, no vehicles, no equipment.
emergency_access.emergency_lane: Maintain minimum 20-foot unobstructed access lane from street to venue interior at all times. Required by Somerville Fire Code for apparatus access.
emergency_access.emergency_exits: Minimum 2 exits for any tent over 200 sq ft. Exits must be clearly marked with illuminated signage. Post exit diagrams at entrance.
emergency_access.nearest_hospital: Cambridge Health Alliance — Somerville Campus, 230 Highland Ave (~0.8 miles)

fire_safety.tent_certification: Tent fabric must carry NFPA 701 flame-retardant certification — require certificate from vendor before booking
fire_safety.open_flame: No propane, open flame, or candles inside tent. Propane cooking stations minimum 10 feet from tent exits.
fire_safety.fire_extinguishers: Minimum 2 ABC extinguishers per 2,500 sq ft of tent area; inspected within 12 months
fire_safety.smoking: Designate smoking area 25+ feet from tent, with provided ash receptacles
fire_safety.utility_poles: 18+ utility poles within 200m — coordinate tent placement to avoid overhead line conflicts

crowd_management.capacity: Post maximum occupancy inside tent (calculate at 6 sq ft per person standing, 10 sq ft per person with seating)
crowd_management.security_ratio: 1 security guard per 100 guests minimum; 2 guards minimum for any event
crowd_management.first_aid: First aid kit on-site; designate a sober first aid liaison for events with alcohol
crowd_management.noise_cutoff: Establish hard stop at 10pm for all amplified sound; security enforces
crowd_management.wristbands: Use color-coded wristbands for alcohol eligibility (21+ check at entry)

cyvl_data_demo_value.insight: Cyvl infrastructure data enabled this party plan to be built without a single site visit. Key data used:
data_points: Exact geocoded coordinates for 76 School St (lat: 42.3852, lon: -71.1000)
50+ pavement segments scored within 300m — identified road condition for guest approach and access planning
8 fire hydrants mapped within 200m — informed safety clearance zones and Fire Prevention Bureau permit narrative
18 utility poles mapped — flagged overhead utility conflict risk for tent placement
50+ signs catalogued — 15+ R7-x no-parking signs revealed that street parking is unavailable, driving the entire guest parking strategy
258 parking lot imagery matches — confirmed visual character of the area
Street-level imagery at 42.3850, -71.1001 shows the exact block (PCI 88.91) — confirming good pavement condition for pedestrian approach
Spring Hill neighborhood identified as Walk Score 92 — validates transit-first parking strategy
